title: Activist investors reap paltry returns from Japanese stockholdings
summary:

fullArticle:
Shares held by funds engaged with management barely outperform broader market

The combined market capitalization of Japanese equities owned by activist investors reached a new record this year. (Photo by Mayumi Tsumita)

TOKYO -- Activist investors are hardly eking out higher returns than the broader Japanese stock market, a recent study shows, suggesting that this may fuel a push for more radical corporate reforms....
